assets.name: lecture_03_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: This is the clearest and most settled frame of the four. The lecturer has moved far enough left that the board is least occluded, while the full pedagogical layout remains visible: a matrix written beside its transpose, the index-level transpose rule at upper right, and the transition toward Hermitian conjugation below. It is the most useful frame for note writing because it preserves both the mathematics and the board organization at the moment symmetric matrices are being explained.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: That's the idea of a symmetric matrix,

assets.name: lecture_03_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: This frame gives the clearest and most useful view of the board at the moment the lecture states that a special vector is returned proportional to itself after matrix action. The eigenvalue-style equation at the top is readable, the diagonal matrix example below is unobstructed, and the lecturer has turned enough toward the board that the mathematics is less visually crowded than in the earlier frames.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: by the matrix, you just get the original vector back

assets.name: lecture_03_figure_04.png
assets.rationale: This frame gives the clearest worked example of the board statement 'matrix times vector equals number times the same vector.' The top eigenvalue relation remains visible, the diagonal matrix and column vector are both legible, and the lecturer's pointing hand highlights the scalar factor without obscuring the crucial entries. It is the most useful frame for note writing because it captures the completed example rather than just the surrounding gesture.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: Matrix times vector equals number times the same vector.

assets.name: lecture_03_figure_05.png
assets.rationale: This is the cleanest frame in the set. The board is unobstructed, the top eigenvalue equation is visible, and the worked product below is shown without the lecturer's hand covering the matrix or vector entries. Although the result has not yet been written on the right, this frame best preserves the setup of the calculation and is therefore the most useful for note writing.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: What is this matrix times this vector?
assets.caption_hint: Off-diagonal matrix acting on a two-component vector
